pkg/framework/entutils/testutils.purpose: Organisational parent for two isolated Ent codegen fixture databases (ent1, ent2) used exclusively to test pkg/framework/entutils transaction helpers (TransactingRepo, HijackTx, savepoints) without touching production openmeter/ent/db. Both children are self-contained: schema → codegen → generated db/ client, each with its own package path scoped under testutils.

patterns.name: Asymmetric extension sets between fixtures
patterns.description: ent1 registers all three framework extensions (entcursor, entexpose, entpaginate); ent2 registers only entexpose. This intentional asymmetry models a minimal second-DB fixture so cross-DB transaction tests can prove the helpers work without cursor/paginate overhead on the second client.
patterns.example: ent1/entc.go: entc.Extensions(entcursor.New(), entexpose.New(), entpaginate.New()) vs ent2/entc.go: entc.Extensions(entexpose.New())

patterns.name: entexpose required on both fixtures
patterns.description: Both ent1 and ent2 must include entexpose.New() so the generated db/ exposes GetConfig(), HijackTx(), NewTxClientFromRawConfig(), and ExposedTxDriver — these are the hooks TransactingRepo uses to rebind ctx-carried transactions to a new typed client.
patterns.example: ent1/db/expose.go: func (c *Client) HijackTx(...) and func NewTxClientFromRawConfig(...)

patterns.name: Ignored-main codegen driver
patterns.description: entc.go carries //go:build ignore so it is excluded from normal compilation; generate.go in the parent package holds the //go:generate go run -mod=mod entc.go directive. Regenerate with go generate ./pkg/framework/entutils/testutils/ent1/... (or make generate).

patterns.name: Schema uses entutils.TimeMixin
patterns.description: Both fixture schemas embed entutils.TimeMixin{} in their Mixin() slice, mirroring how production schemas use shared mixins. Any new test schema added here must include this mixin.

patterns.name: Package path scoped under testutils
patterns.description: entc.go Package field must resolve to the correct testutils sub-path. Using the wrong path overwrites the wrong generated package.

patterns.name: Standard four Ent features on both fixtures
patterns.description: Both entc.go files declare the same four gen.Feature values: FeatureVersionedMigration, FeatureLock, FeatureUpsert, FeatureExecQuery. Removing any diverges test clients from production capabilities and breaks transaction-level test assertions.

key_file_guides.role: Codegen driver for ent1 fixture; registers all three framework extensions and all four features. Run via go generate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add production domain imports; never change Target/Package to production paths.

key_file_guides.role: Codegen driver for ent2 fixture; intentionally minimal — only entexpose extension. Must stay lightweight.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add entcursor or entpaginate — ent2 is the minimal second-DB fixture by design.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by entexpose; exposes HijackTx, NewTxClientFromRawConfig, ExposedTxDriver. DO NOT EDIT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Primary integration surface for TransactingRepo savepoint tests; changes require regeneration, not hand-edits.

key_file_guides.role: Generated helper that opens a db.Client and auto-runs schema migration via migrate.Tables. Used in tests to bootstrap a fresh test-postgres DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT; regenerate. enttest.Open/NewClient auto-migrate using ent1 migrate.Tables.

key_file_guides.role: Minimal fixture schema with TimeMixin, string id, and one value field. Source of truth for what ent1 generates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep minimal; do not import production domain packages.

key_file_guides.role: Parallel minimal fixture schema for ent2 second-DB client.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep minimal; do not share schema types with ent1 — they represent independent DBs.

anti_patterns: Pointing Target or Package in entc.go at production openmeter/ent/db paths — overwrites production generated code
Adding entcursor or entpaginate extensions to ent2 — destroys the intentional asymmetry and fattens the minimal fixture
Removing any of the four standard gen.Feature flags from either fixture — diverges test-generated client from production capabilities
Importing production domain packages (openmeter/billing, openmeter/customer, etc.) into schema/ or entc.go files
Hand-editing any file under ent1/db/ or ent2/db/ — all are generated; re-run go generate instead

decisions.decision: Two separate fixture databases (ent1 + ent2) instead of one
decisions.rationale: TransactingRepo and HijackTx tests must prove cross-DB transaction propagation; a single fixture cannot exercise the multi-client path that production code uses when queries span DB boundaries.

decisions.decision: ent1 carries all three framework extensions; ent2 carries only entexpose
decisions.rationale: ent1 exercises the full framework surface (cursor, paginate, expose); ent2 is intentionally minimal to isolate second-client transaction behavior without pulling in unrelated generated helpers.

decisions.decision: Fixtures live under pkg/framework/entutils/testutils, not under openmeter/*/testutils
decisions.rationale: The tested code is in pkg/framework/entutils; placing fixtures here avoids import cycles with domain packages and keeps test scaffolding co-located with the framework code it validates.

code_examples.scenario: How entexpose's HijackTx is used by TransactingRepo to rebind a ctx-carried transaction to a typed ent1 client
